commit.message:
Add full finance toolkit — 7 tools in one app

- app.py: home page with tool overview
- pages/1: 5-Year Financial Model (moved from root)
- pages/2: Annual Budget (existing)
- pages/3: Lending Calculator (loan + mortgage with early repayment)
- pages/4: Portfolio Analysis (Sharpe, VaR, risk/return scatter)
- pages/5: Stock Comparison (2 stocks, correlation, ratios)
- pages/6: Commodities Dashboard (gold, silver, oil, gas, copper, wheat)
- pages/7: Stock Analysis (any ticker, MAs, volatility, company info)

All charts rebuilt in Plotly. Consistent styling across all pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Annual Budget tool (1-year P&L with monthly breakdown)

- Sales forecast, direct costs, overheads by category
- Monthly P&L table with FY totals and % of revenue
- KPI cards: Revenue, Gross Profit, Overheads, EBITDA
- 4 charts: Revenue vs Costs, Monthly EBITDA, Overhead breakdown, Cumulative EBITDA
- Excel template download + upload
- PDF export with branded report

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: 5-year financial model

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: hot reload TOML config on file changes (#261)

* feat: hot reload TOML config on file changes

Watch dbhub.toml for changes using fs.watch and automatically reload
database connections and tool registry without requiring a server restart.
Includes rollback to previous config if reconnection fails.

Closes #257

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review feedback on config watcher

- Handle rename events for atomic editor writes (vim, VS Code)
- Add watcher.unref() to prevent keeping process alive
- Re-establish watch on error (file replaced by atomic rename)
- Use process.on("exit") instead of SIGINT/SIGTERM to avoid
  overriding Node's default signal behavior in HTTP mode
- Add reloadPending flag to avoid dropping file changes during reload
- Store full old config (sources + tools) for complete rollback
- Document STDIO transport limitation for tool list updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address second round of review feedback

- Initialize lastGoodTools from startup config via options parameter
- Disconnect partial state before rollback to avoid leaked connections
- Extract watcher creation into recursive helper for consistent re-watch
- Replace slow real-timer test with fast fake-timer tests for new cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: initialize watcher as null for definite assignment safety

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: wrap fs.watch in try/catch and add watcher re-establish test

- Wrap initial fs.watch call in try/catch to handle missing file gracefully
- Separate close from re-create in error handler for cleaner error paths
- Add test verifying watcher re-establishes on error events

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: log accurate hot reload status when watcher setup fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add delayed retry for watcher re-creation with stopped guard

- Re-create watcher via setTimeout (1s delay) to avoid tight loops
- Add stopped flag to prevent retries after cleanup
- Set stopped=true in cleanup function to halt pending retries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: simplify config watcher — remove rename/retry over-engineering

Strip back to essentials: only handle "change" events from fs.watch.
Remove rename event handling, watcher re-creation retry logic, stopped
flag, and nullable watcher type. The core use case is detecting file
content changes, not handling atomic renames or transient file absence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add hot reload section to TOML configuration docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Reduce ablation subset from 15% to 5% of total data

5% provides ~136K NTP + ~13K QA samples — sufficient for ~2 NTP epochs
and reliable QA convergence within 2000 steps, while cutting data prep
and training time by 3x.

commit.message:
Fix Reddit scoring: question/high-value bonus requires keyword match to fire

Previously any question from r/realestate scored 4 (2 question + 2 high-value)
regardless of keywords, creating noise from unrelated real estate posts.
Now both bonuses only apply when at least one T1/T2 keyword matched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

data: unblock 3 potentials (C2, C3, G1) + update seed to Piano 14

Results:
- C2 (zeta validation): log pattern r=0.92 (p=5.6e-42), prime r=-0.03.
  K_c correlates with t_n only when energy levels follow log scale —
  matching the density of zeta zeros (ln(t)/2pi). Scale matching, not
  individual primes, drives the bridge.
- C3 (topological charge): chi_DND in [-0.043, -0.012], all near zero.
  Quantization not observed in this regime.
- G1 (Bloch explorer): phi-dist=0.021 best, 2/20 scans within 0.05.
  Weak emergence, not a robust lock-in.

Seed updated: N1/BOUNDARY resolved, C2/C3/G1 executed.
Direction: investigate why log pattern produces r=0.92 (density matching).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(WF-CRM-PROXY): n8n IF 노드 라우팅 버그 수정 + CRM 프록시 완전 동작

주요 수정:
- queryParametersJson → jsonQuery 필드명 수정 (n8n v4.2 HTTP Request 노드 호환)
- IF 조건 boolean 비교 대신 _status !== 200 숫자 비교로 변경 (n8n v2 boolean bug 우회)
- IF connection 순서 재정렬: output[0]=error→RespondError, output[1]=ok→NocoDB
- docker-compose.yml CRM_API_KEY 환경변수 추가 → n8n 컨테이너 재시작

검증 완료:
- 인증 실패 401 UNAUTHORIZED ✓
- 읽기 전용 403 READ_ONLY ✓
- 허용 안 된 테이블 400 INVALID_TABLE ✓
- 고객 조회 200 OK (15830 rows) ✓
- 거래내역 조회 200 OK (97086 rows) ✓

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build remaining portfolio projects: Meal Planner, Photo Workflow OS, AI Bullet Journal

Meal Planner: ingredient input -> 3 meal cards with recipes, Big Green Egg
mode for grill temps and wood pairings. Demo detects ingredient types.

Photo Workflow OS (flagship): 6-stage kanban pipeline (Inquiry -> Quote ->
Contract -> Prep -> Delivery -> Complete). Drag-and-drop cards between stages.
AI generates quotes, contracts, shot lists, delivery emails, and follow-up
plans. localStorage persistence.

AI Bullet Journal (flagship): split-panel chat + journal. Natural language
input parsed into typed cards (task, event, note, reflection, goal). Day and
week views, task checkboxes, goal progress bars, BuJo-inspired design with
Caveat handwriting font. localStorage persistence.

All projects: demo mode works without API key, live Claude API integration
when key provided, portfolio design system (Space Mono, Outfit, #d97757).

commit.message:
disable asyncpg prepared statement cache for Supabase pgbouncer

Supabase connection pooler uses pgbouncer in transaction mode which
does not support prepared statements. Setting statement_cache_size=0
and prepared_statement_cache_size=0 in connect_args fixes the
DuplicatePreparedStatementError.

commit.message:
refactor: move benchmarks to separate crate (#148)

## Summary

Moves benchmarks from `lance-graph` to a new `lance-graph-benches` crate
to exclude them from release binaries and reduce build times for
production code.

## Changes

### New Crate: `lance-graph-benches`
- Created dedicated benchmark crate with `publish = false`
- Moved `graph_execution` benchmark from `lance-graph`
- Added README with usage instructions

### Cleaned Up `lance-graph`
- ✅ Removed benchmark-only dependencies:
  - `criterion` (benchmarking framework)
  - `lance-index` (unused)
- ✅ Kept test dependencies:
  - `tempfile` (used by tests)
  - `lance-arrow` (used by tests)
  - `tokio`, `futures` (used by tests)
- ✅ Removed `[[bench]]` section from Cargo.toml

### Workspace
- Added `lance-graph-benches` to workspace members

## Benefits

✅ **Smaller Release Binaries** - Benchmark code excluded from published
crates
✅ **Faster Production Builds** - Benchmarks only compiled when
explicitly requested
✅ **Cleaner Separation** - Dev/benchmark code separate from production
code
✅ **All Tests Pass** - 295 unit tests still passing  
✅ **Benchmarks Work** - Verified compilation and structure

## Usage

```bash
# Run all benchmarks
cargo bench -p lance-graph-benches

# Run specific benchmark
cargo bench -p lance-graph-benches --bench graph_execution

# Build production crate (benchmarks NOT included)
cargo build -p lance-graph
```

## Testing

- ✅ `cargo check -p lance-graph` - success
- ✅ `cargo build -p lance-graph` - success
- ✅ `cargo test -p lance-graph --lib` - 295 tests pass
- ✅ `cargo check -p lance-graph-benches` - success
- ✅ No benchmark code in production binary

## File Structure

```
crates/lance-graph-benches/
├── benches/
│   └── graph_execution.rs
├── Cargo.toml (publish = false)
└── README.md
```

This follows the common Rust pattern of separating benchmarks into
dedicated crates when they have heavy dependencies or shouldn't be
included in releases.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: provide IndexSearcher to SearchResultHandler via Spring DI

## Problem
SearchConfiguration was passing null for IndexSearcher parameter,
causing NullPointerException at runtime when SearchResultHandler.executeSearch()
tried to execute Lucene queries against the index.

## Solution
1. Added getSearcher() method to FileIndexer that:
   - Commits pending changes to IndexWriter
   - Opens/manages a cached DirectoryReader
   - Returns IndexSearcher for querying the index
   - Automatically refreshes cache when index changes

2. Created IndexConfiguration class to register FileIndexer as a Spring bean

3. Updated SearchConfiguration to:
   - Add indexSearcher() bean that obtains IndexSearcher from FileIndexer
   - Inject IndexSearcher into searchResultHandler() instead of passing null
   - All dependencies now properly wired

## Testing
All 128 tests pass including:
- SearchControllerTest (15 tests)
- SearchIntegrationTest (15 tests)
- SearchResultHandlerTest (14 tests)
- All other component tests

## Documentation
Updated LESSONS_LEARNED.md with issue and solution

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(FFS-141): Add data quality regression monitoring

- New migration MIG_2813 extending ops.check_entity_linking_health() with 3 checks:
  duplicate_places, unpropagated_matches, mislinked_appointments
- Add duplicate_places, unpropagated_matches, mislinked_appointments to
  /api/health/entity-linking response and isHealthy condition
- Add duplicate_place_groups, unpropagated_matches, mislinked_appointments to
  /api/cron/data-quality-check metrics and alert thresholds
- Add console.warn in ingest route when entity linking links 0 in both steps
- Include previously staged ingest improvements (owner change detection, function rename)

Fixes FFS-141
